inclusion_criteria: Population: Studies involving dental practitioners (e.g., dentists, hygienists, assistants), dental office staff, dental patients, dental students, or dental educators within dental care settings (e.g., clinics, dental hospitals, dental school clinics, community dental services).

Exposure/Intervention: Assess sexual misconduct in dental settings (e.g., harassment, assault, boundary violations) or evaluate interventions/policies/programs targeting sexual misconduct behaviors; comparator of non-exposed individuals/settings when applicable but not required.

Outcomes: Report at least one of the following—prevalence/incidence of sexual misconduct, underreporting/reporting behaviors, emotional or psychological impacts (e.g., trauma), professional disengagement or related outcomes (e.g., turnover, withdrawal), or effectiveness of interventions.

Study design: Primary empirical research (quantitative, qualitative, mixed-methods, or interventional), including surveys, observational studies, interviews/focus groups, trials, or program evaluations.
exclusion_criteria: Populations or settings outside dentistry (e.g., general healthcare with no dental subgroup) or non-dental workplaces/educational settings.
Not focused on sexual misconduct (e.g., general professionalism, bullying, discrimination without a sexual component) or cannot isolate dental sexual misconduct outcomes.
Non-empirical publications (editorials, opinion pieces, narrative commentary, legal case summaries) or single-patient case reports without analytic data.
Do not report any of the specified outcomes (prevalence/underreporting/emotional trauma/professional disengagement/intervention effectiveness).
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies on sexual misconduct within dental care settings, including prevalence, reporting, harms, professional impacts, and intervention effectiveness; include empirical studies with relevant dental populations.

included_articles.title: Sexual harassment in dentistry: prevalence in dental school.
Sexual Harassment Issues Among Virginia Dental Hygienists.
An International Survey of Female Dental Students' Perceptions About Gender Bias and Sexual Misconduct at Four Dental Schools.
Inappropriate Patient Sexual Behavior in the Dental Practice Setting: Experiences of dental hygienists.
